message:
fix(core): eagerly initialize DB schema during HTTP and TUI startup

Both `run_http()` and `run_http_with_tui()` now call `readiness_check()`
immediately after the probe report passes. This forces SQLite migrations
to run before any TUI screen or HTTP handler opens a direct sync DB
connection, preventing "no such table" query errors that could occur
when the database file existed but had not yet been migrated.

Previously, the first TUI refresh or API call that touched a sync DB
handle could race against lazy initialization and observe an empty
schema. The new eager init makes this impossible.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
